title: sf-security-review-toolkit audit ledger
description: Schema for <target>/.security-review/audit-ledger.json — the audit engine's memory across passes (audit-methodology.md §5). The ledger is updated MECHANICALLY by engine code, never by an LLM agent: a synthesis agent paraphrasing entries corrupts the dedup keys. Entries enter the ledger only after the adversarial verify stage; unverified candidates never land here (audit-methodology.md §3.3). No field may contain a secret value — the engine redacts credential material in `evidence` snippets ('***redacted***') before writing (CONVENTIONS §6). Recommend committing this file: the ledger digest is what makes pass N+1 cheap and quiet.

schema_version.description: Ledger format version. Bump only with a migration note in this schema's $comment history.

pass_id.description: 1-based ordinal of a pass; matches the <N> in docs/security-review/audit-report-<date>-pass<N>.md and the run-log entries.

lens.description: Track-1b: one dimension's view of a cross-dimension merged finding (audit-methodology.md §5.2). Carries that dimension's own severity/verdict/reasoning/evidence so an incremental re-run can refresh a single lens. Since 0.8.102 a lens also carries its own provenance quartet (provenance/engine/ruleId/class, copied verbatim from the pre-merge finding when present) so the cross-dimension collapse is information-preserving: a merged deterministic lens stays dispositionable through harness/apply-dispositions.mjs, which matches lenses by exact engine+ruleId.

provenance.description: This lens's own provenance, copied verbatim from the pre-merge finding (0.8.102). Absent when the pre-merge finding carried none (pre-provenance-era rows default to llm-inferred).

engine.description: The deterministic engine behind THIS lens (0.8.102). Present iff the lens's pre-merge finding carried one; apply-dispositions matches lenses by exact engine+ruleId.

ruleId.description: The scanner/metadata rule behind THIS lens (0.8.102), recorded verbatim for reviewer reproducibility and exact disposition matching.

class.description: The toolkit's owned-class label for THIS lens (0.8.102), copied verbatim from the pre-merge deterministic finding when its rule mapped to a known class.

trace_step.description: One location on a scanner-computed dataflow trace (B5 · E0.1): the repo-relative file and 1-based line of a source, intermediate, or sink step. Location only — the step's matched source text is intentionally not recorded.

id.description: Stable dedup key, computed mechanically — NEVER hand-edited and NEVER derived from the description (descriptions are model prose and differ across runs; see audit-methodology.md §5.2). Hash inputs, exactly: (1) normalized_file = repo-relative file path with any trailing ':<line>' suffix stripped (line numbers drift with unrelated edits); (2) normalized_title = title lowercased, runs of whitespace collapsed to a single space, all characters outside [a-z0-9 ] removed, trimmed. id = first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(normalized_file + '\n' + normalized_title). Two candidates with the same id are the same finding for merge purposes; near-misses across dimensions stay separate entries and merge only in the report.

dimension.description: Key of the dimension whose finder raised it — the filename stem under ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/methodology/dimensions/ (e.g. 'oauth-identity', 'tenant-isolation', 'sessionid-egress', 'mcp-threat-model'). Kebab-case by convention; not an enum so a new dimension file does not require a schema bump.

title.description: Finder's title. Frozen once written — editing it changes the dedup key derivation and orphans the entry.

severity.description: The FINDER's severity hypothesis. Retained as provenance only — adjusted_severity is authoritative everywhere downstream (audit-methodology.md §2.3).

adjusted_severity.description: The VERIFIER's severity. Sorts the report, drives the readiness gates, and is the value compile-submission reads.

file.description: path:line of the flagged code at the time of the finding. The :line part is display metadata — it is stripped before hashing into id, so refactors don't resurrect old findings.

status.description: Lifecycle state (audit-methodology.md §5.1). confirmed: verifier returned confirmed_real or partially_real — open, counts against the readiness gates, in the do-not-re-report digest. refuted: verifier returned false_positive — kept (never deleted) so the next pass's finder doesn't re-raise it, and its reasoning is reusable verbatim in the FP dossier when a scanner later flags the same pattern. fixed: remediated with a fix reference; re-reported by a later pass only as a regression (the merge flips it back to confirmed with regression: true). accepted_risk: the partner decided, with a written justification, not to fix — dispositioned for gate purposes like a dossier entry, but unlike refuted the defect is real; it must carry accepted_risk_justification and appears in the readiness tracker so the acceptance is visible, not buried. superseded: an llm-inferred finding that a deterministic engine finding made redundant — a deterministic finding in the SAME owned class at the SAME locus is authoritative, so the LLM finding is moved out of the open band (docs/roadmap-deterministic-findings.md §3; harness/reconcile-provenance.mjs). Kept (never deleted) with superseded_by pointing at the deterministic finding's id, so the supersession is auditable and recoverable — the LLM never refutes/downgrades/duplicates what an engine already determined. Only an llm-inferred finding is ever superseded; a deterministic finding is never.

class.description: The toolkit's OWNED-CLASS label for a deterministic finding (docs/roadmap-deterministic-findings.md §4): the fine-grained class key the scanner/metadata engine re-homes (e.g. `crud-fls`, `sharing`, `viewall-overgrant`) — distinct from the coarser methodology `dimension`. Present ONLY on a deterministic finding whose rule mapped to a known class; an unmapped-fallback deterministic finding and every llm-inferred finding omit it. harness/reconcile-provenance.mjs reads this: a deterministic finding supersedes a co-located LLM finding only in a class it OWNS (a finding without `class` owns none and supersedes nothing).

superseded_by.description: Set on a finding with status `superseded`: the `id` of the deterministic finding that made this llm-inferred finding redundant (same owned class + overlapping locus). Engine-maintained by harness/reconcile-provenance.mjs; do not hand-edit.

superseded_reason.description: Set on a finding with status `superseded`: a one-line human note recording which deterministic engine/rule/locus superseded it. Provenance for the audit trail — the LLM finding is not deleted, it is dispositioned.

disposition_reason.description: Set on a `deterministic` finding whose lifecycle status was flipped by a structured class adjudication in .security-review/deterministic-dispositions.json (harness/apply-dispositions.mjs): a one-line auditable note recording the engine/ruleId dispositioned and the adjudication reason — the SAME reason the false-positive dossier row carries (single source, so the dossier and the ledger can never diverge). The finding's provenance/engine/ruleId/class/severity stay intact; only the lifecycle status moved (to `refuted` or `accepted_risk`, never into the open band, never `fixed`). Engine-maintained; do not hand-edit.

partial_disposition.description: Set on a deterministic cross-dimension MERGED parent when dispositions matched SOME but not ALL of its lenses (harness/apply-dispositions.mjs, 0.8.102): a one-line auditable note naming which lens engine/ruleIds were matched. The parent's status is NOT changed — a merged parent is a conjunction of observations and is flipped out of the open band only when EVERY lens is matched (a partial flip would let a real finding hide behind a co-located false positive). Engine-maintained; do not hand-edit.

pending_readjudication.description: Set on a `deterministic` finding that matches a rule-wide (`scope.as_of_pass`) adjudication by exact engine+ruleId but whose own `first_seen` post-dates that adjudication — or is missing/invalid (fail closed: a finding we cannot date is a finding we have not adjudicated) — (harness/apply-dispositions.mjs, 0.8.103): a one-line auditable note recording which adjudication it matched and that this NEW locus must be re-adjudicated. The status is NOT changed — the finding stays in the open band, the headline, and the blocker count; a disposition may never suppress a finding nobody has looked at. Cleared by the engine when a later in-scope disposition legitimately flips the finding. Engine-maintained; do not hand-edit.

first_seen.description: Pass id in which this finding first entered the ledger.

last_seen.description: Most recent pass whose find or verify stage touched this entry (re-confirmed, re-refuted, or regression-matched).

verdict.description: The verifier's raw verdict (VERDICT_SCHEMA, audit-methodology.md §3.2), kept as provenance — 'partially_real' distinguishes a real-but-overstated defect from a fully confirmed one even though both map to status: confirmed.

verdict_reasoning.description: The verifier's reasoning: what it read in the actual code that confirms or refutes the finding. For refuted entries this text is the seed of the false-positive dossier entry.

evidence.description: Exact file:line + code snippet that decided the verdict. Secret values MUST be redacted by the engine before write.

exploit_scenario.description: Attacker, request, impact — carried from the finder for confirmed entries so the report and remediation plan don't lose it.

recommendation.description: Finder's remediation recommendation, possibly tightened by synthesis.

resolution_note.description: One line for the {{LEDGER}} digest: the fix summary, the refute reason, or the acceptance summary. Keep it short — the digest is prompt overhead multiplied by every finder agent in a pass (audit-methodology.md §5.3).

fix_commit.description: Commit hash or PR reference of the remediation. Required when status is fixed. Set by the user or by a skill that verified the fix actually landed — never on the partner's word alone (CONVENTIONS §2).

accepted_risk_justification.description: Required when status is accepted_risk: why the partner accepts this real defect (compensating control, unreachable precondition in their deployment, scheduled post-submission fix). A bare acceptance with no justification is the ledger equivalent of an undocumented N/A.

accepted_by.description: Who signed off on the acceptance (name/role) and when — acceptance is an owner decision, never agent-made.

regression.description: True when a fixed entry was re-matched by a later pass's candidate and flipped back to confirmed (audit-methodology.md §5.1). Cleared when re-fixed.

merged_dimensions.description: Track-1b (audit-methodology.md §5.2). When ONE root cause is independently found under multiple dimensions at the SAME file+location, merge-ledger collapses them into ONE entry at the highest verified adjusted_severity. This is the sorted list (length >= 2) of every dimension whose lens contributes. Absent on ordinary single-dimension findings. The top-level `dimension`/`title`/`id` are the highest-severity lens's; `adjusted_severity` is the max; per-lens detail lives in `lenses` and the labelled `verdict_reasoning`/`evidence`. Maintained mechanically (explode → per-id merge → collapseCrossDimension); do not hand-edit.

lenses.description: Track-1b: the per-dimension detail of a cross-dimension merged entry (one object per contributing dimension), so an incremental re-run can update one lens without losing the others' reasoning. Present iff `merged_dimensions` is. Engine-maintained; do not hand-edit.

provenance.description: How this finding came to exist (docs/roadmap-deterministic-findings.md §3). `deterministic`: produced MECHANICALLY by a scanner/metadata engine via harness/ingest-scanner-findings.mjs — relayed verbatim, the LLM never re-judges its existence or severity; `engine` + `ruleId` are set and `adjusted_severity` is fixed from the requirement-class map, NOT a per-instance LLM call. `llm-inferred`: reasoned by an LLM finder/verifier over a deterministic fact substrate. DEFAULT `llm-inferred` for back-compat — a finding written before this field existed validates unchanged and is treated as llm-inferred. On a cross-dimension MERGED parent (0.8.102) provenance is the CONJUNCTION of its lenses: `deterministic` iff EVERY lens is deterministic, else `llm-inferred` — always set by the collapse, never absent. NOTE the id derivation differs by provenance: an llm-inferred id hashes file+title (line stripped; titles are model prose that drift); a deterministic id hashes engine+ruleId+file:line (one entry per scanner violation at one sink location, so N distinct violations of one rule in a file stay N findings).

engine.description: The deterministic engine that produced this finding (e.g. `pmd`, `sfge`, `metadata`). Present iff `provenance` is `deterministic` — EXCEPT on a cross-dimension MERGED parent (0.8.102): the parent carries `engine` only when every lens agrees on the value; when lenses disagree (e.g. bandit + detect-secrets at one locus) the field is omitted on the parent and the per-lens records are the authority. Absent on llm-inferred findings.

ruleId.description: The scanner/metadata rule that fired (e.g. `ApexCRUDViolation`, `DatabaseOperationsMustUseWithSharing`, `viewall-overgrant`). Present iff `provenance` is `deterministic` — with the same merged-parent exception as `engine` (0.8.102): present on a merged parent only when every lens agrees; the LLM never invents one. Recorded verbatim so the finding is reviewer-reproducible against the same scanner run.

reachable.description: True when the producing scanner PROVED a source→sink dataflow path for this finding (Semgrep/Opengrep taint-mode JSON — B5 · E0.1; SARIF codeFlows from any engine — B5 · E0.2b). Present iff `reachabilityPath` is. ABSENCE means the scanner recorded no path — 'not analyzed', never 'unreachable'; no finding carries `reachable: false`.

reachabilityPath.description: The scanner-computed source→sink dataflow trace, normalized by harness/ingest-scanner-findings.mjs from Semgrep/Opengrep taint-mode `extra.dataflow_trace` (B5 · E0.1) or from the standardized SARIF 2.1.0 `codeFlows` taint path of any producing engine — opengrep, semgrep-Pro, CodeQL (B5 · E0.2b, one normal form across engines and formats): the untrusted-input source location, the ordered intermediate propagation steps (may be empty), and the sensitive-sink location. Relayed MECHANICALLY from captured scanner output — never LLM-authored, never entering the finding's id/severity/band. Locations only; matched source text is never recorded here. Present iff `reachable` is.

audited_commit.description: git HEAD (full or short SHA) of the partner repo at the time this pass ran — the resumption fingerprint. On resume, `git diff --name-only <audited_commit> HEAD` identifies findings whose files changed since the audit; those are flagged potentially-stale (re-audit before trusting the verdict), never silently trusted. Recorded by audit-codebase; consumed by the journey preflight and compile-submission (harness/ledger-staleness.mjs). New passes SHOULD set it — this is enforced by the audit-codebase skill instruction, NOT by this schema (it is intentionally absent from `required` so ledgers written before the field still validate). A latest pass that lacks it is treated as `no-fingerprint` (staleness cannot be verified — re-audit), never silently trusted against an older pass's commit.

tier.description: Token tier the pass ran at (audit-methodology.md §7). The readiness verdict records which tier and how many passes produced the ledger it reads — a quick-tier ledger is a triage, not an audit.

dimensions.description: Dimension keys this pass actually ran (post target-map vetoes and N/A markings) — not the ones planned.

agents.description: Agent counts actually launched — the honest cost record behind the tier estimates.

candidates.description: Raw findings emitted by finders this pass, pre-verification.

unverified.description: Candidates whose verifier failed twice — logged to the run log per audit-methodology.md §3.3, never reported and never ledgered as findings. Tracked here so the count isn't silently lost.

coverage_failed.description: Dimension keys whose FINDER crashed this pass (the finder agent hit the StructuredOutput retry cap — workflow-template's agent() returns null — or its whole pipeline result was dropped by a thrown stage). Coverage is INCOMPLETE for these, NOT clean: they produced no findings because they did not run, not because nothing is there (BUG-A). The recap surfaces 'coverage incomplete — re-run X' and the pass is never `dry` while this is non-empty, so a crashed finder can never read as a clean verdict or count toward the stop rule. Re-run these dimensions (audit-codebase step 8). Absent/empty when every finder completed; intentionally NOT in `required` so ledgers written before 0.8.44 still validate.

dry.description: True when the pass confirmed zero NEW findings at severity low or above (new info entries don't count). Two consecutive dry passes — the second being a full-band pass — is the engine's stop rule (audit-methodology.md §6). Dry means 'this method found nothing new', never 'nothing is there'.

report_path.description: Repo-relative path of the synthesis report, e.g. docs/security-review/audit-report-2026-06-12-pass2.md.
